reasoning: At 8x zoom the flame/glow arrowhead tip shows multi-tone gradient-like transitions rather than 2-3 hard pixel tones — the white core bleeds into yellow then orange with soft intermediate shades suggesting generative blending rather than deliberate pixel placement. The sky-blue icon background is not flat: scattered lighter and darker blue pixels create low-level noise in what should be a solid fill. The outline of the rounded-rect card border wavers between 1px and 2px thickness, most visible at the top-left and bottom-right corners. The flame plume leans slightly left, breaking the expected vertical symmetry of an upward arrow. The fletching (bottom feathers) details partially melt into the shaft — individual vane shapes are ambiguous at 8x. No outright semantic failure (it reads as a flaming arrow projectile icon), and the overall pixel-block structure is intact, preventing an outright fail, but the accumulation of soft gradient tells and noise in solid regions is inconsistent with clean hand-crafted pixel art.
